SpecimenTypeEnum.name: SpecimenTypeEnum
SpecimenTypeEnum.description: A high-level type of specimen, based on its derivation provenance (i.e. how far removed it is from the original sample extracted from a source).

ALIQUOT.description: A specimen that results from the division of some parent specimen into equal amounts for downstream analysis.

ANALYTE.description: A specimen generated through the extraction of a specified class of substance/chemical (e.g. DNA, RNA, protein) from a parent specimen, which is stored in solution as an analyte.

FRESH_SPECIMEN.description: A specimen representing the material that was directly collected from a subject (i.e. not generated through portioning, aliquoting, or analyte extraction from an existing specimen).

PORTION.description: A physical sub-part taken from an existing specimen.

SLIDE.description: A specimen that is mounted on a slide or coverslip for microscopic analysis.

ADDITIONAL_METASTATIC.description: A metastatic tumor sample collected in addition to a previously collected metastatic specimen.

ADDITIONAL_NEW_PRIMARY.description: A new primary tumor sample collected in addition to a previously collected primary specimen.

BENIGN_NEOPLASMS.description: Tissue derived from a non-cancerous tumor that does not invade nearby tissue or metastasize.

BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_BONE_MARROW.description: Cancerous cells isolated from bone marrow as part of a blood-derived cancer sample.

BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_BONE_MARROW_POST_TR.description: Cancerous cells isolated from bone marrow following therapeutic intervention.

BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_PERIPHERAL_BLOOD.description: Cancerous cells isolated from peripheral blood as part of a blood-derived cancer sample.

BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_PERIPHERAL_BLOOD_PO.description: Cancerous cells isolated from peripheral blood following therapeutic intervention.

BLOOD_DERIVED_LIQUID_BIOPSY.description: Tumor-derived material, such as circulating tumor DNA or cells, extracted from a blood sample.

BLOOD_DERIVED_NORMAL.description: Non-cancerous blood sample used as a normal reference for genomic or molecular analyses.

BONE_MARROW_NORMAL.description: Non-cancerous bone marrow sample used as a normal reference for genomic or molecular analyses.

BUCCAL_CELL_NORMAL.description: Normal cells collected from the inner cheek mucosa, typically used as a germline reference.

CELL_LINE_DERIVED_XENOGRAFT_TISSUE.description: Tumor tissue derived from a xenograft model that was originally established from a cultured cell line.

CELL_LINES.description: Immortalized cells grown in culture, derived from tumor or normal tissue for experimental use.

CONTROL_ANALYTE.description: A reference material used to validate or calibrate laboratory assays and analytical processes.

DNA.description: Deoxyribonucleic acid extracted from a biological sample for genomic analysis.

EBV_IMMORTALIZED_NORMAL.description: Normal B-lymphocytes immortalized by Epstein-Barr virus transformation, used as a germline reference.

EXPANDED_NEXT_GENERATION_CANCER_MODEL.description: A cancer model, such as an organoid or cell line, that has been propagated or expanded from a next-generation model system.

FFPE_RECURRENT.description: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue from a recurrent tumor, preserved for histological or molecular analysis.

FFPE_SCROLLS.description: Thin sections cut from formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue blocks for nucleic acid or protein extraction.

FIBROBLASTS_FROM_BONE_MARROW_NORMAL.description: Normal fibroblast cells isolated from bone marrow, used as a non-cancerous reference.

GRANULOCYTES.description: White blood cells of the granulocyte lineage isolated from blood, often used as a normal germline reference.

HUMAN_TUMOR_ORIGINAL_CELLS.description: Primary tumor cells taken directly from a human subject without prior in vitro expansion.

IN_SITU_NEOPLASMS.description: Tissue from a neoplasm that has not yet invaded surrounding tissue, representing an early-stage malignancy.

LYMPHOID_NORMAL.description: Non-cancerous lymphoid tissue used as a normal reference for genomic or molecular analyses.

METASTATIC.description: Tumor tissue collected from a site distant from the primary tumor, representing metastatic disease.

MIXED_ADHERENT_SUSPENSION.description: A cell culture sample containing both adherent and suspension cell populations.

MONONUCLEAR_CELLS_FROM_BONE_MARROW_NORMA.description: Normal mononuclear cells isolated from bone marrow, used as a non-cancerous reference.

NEOPLASMS_OF_UNCERTAIN_AND_UNKNOWN_BEHAV.description: Tissue from a neoplasm whose malignant potential cannot be determined based on available information.

NEXT_GENERATION_CANCER_MODEL.description: A modern in vitro or in vivo cancer model, such as a patient-derived organoid or xenograft, that recapitulates tumor biology.

NORMAL_ADJACENT_TISSUE.description: Histologically normal tissue collected from a site adjacent to a tumor, used as a local reference.

NOT_ALLOWED_TO_COLLECT.description: A sample that was not obtained because collection was prohibited by consent, regulation, or protocol.

NOT_REPORTED.description: The source material type was not documented or reported for this sample.

PLEURAL_EFFUSION.description: Fluid collected from the pleural cavity, which may contain tumor cells in the context of malignancy.

POST_NEOADJUVANT_THERAPY.description: Tumor tissue collected after the administration of neoadjuvant (pre-surgical) therapy.

PRIMARY_BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_BONE_MARROW.description: The initial cancerous bone marrow sample collected from a patient with a blood-derived malignancy.

PRIMARY_BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_PERIPHERAL_.description: The initial cancerous peripheral blood sample collected from a patient with a blood-derived malignancy.

PRIMARY_TUMOR.description: Tissue collected from the original site of tumor origin, prior to any metastatic spread.

PRIMARY_XENOGRAFT_TISSUE.description: Tumor tissue derived from a first-generation xenograft established directly from a patient sample.

RECURRENT_BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_BONE_MARR.description: Cancerous bone marrow sample collected at the time of disease recurrence in a blood-derived malignancy.

RECURRENT_BLOOD_DERIVED_CANCER_PERIPHERA.description: Cancerous peripheral blood sample collected at the time of disease recurrence in a blood-derived malignancy.

RECURRENT_TUMOR.description: Tumor tissue collected following disease recurrence after prior treatment or remission.

RNA.description: Ribonucleic acid extracted from a biological sample for transcriptomic or gene expression analysis.

SALIVA.description: Saliva collected as a non-invasive source of DNA or other biological material, typically for germline analysis.

SLIDES.description: Prepared microscopy slides containing tissue or cell specimens for histological or molecular examination.

SOLID_TISSUE_NORMAL.description: Non-cancerous solid tissue used as a normal reference for genomic or molecular analyses.

TOTAL_RNA.description: The complete RNA complement extracted from a sample, including messenger, ribosomal, and non-coding RNA species.

TUMOR.description: Tissue derived from a malignant neoplasm, used broadly when more specific tumor classification is not specified.

TUMOR_ADJACENT_NORMAL_POST_NEOADJUVANT_T.description: Histologically normal tissue adjacent to a tumor, collected after completion of neoadjuvant therapy.

UNKNOWN.description: The source material type is not known or could not be determined for this sample.

XENOGRAFT_TISSUE.description: Tumor tissue derived from a xenograft model in which human tumor cells are propagated in an animal host.

SectionLocationEnum.name: SectionLocationEnum
SectionLocationEnum.description: The location in a parent specimen from which a section/portion was excised.

BOTTOM.description: The part of a specimen designated as its 'bottom' based on specified orientation criteria.

TOP.description: The part of a specimen designated as its 'top' based on specified orientation criteria.

UNKNOWN.description: An unknown location on a specimen.

SpecimenCreationActivityTypeEnum.name: SpecimenCreationActivityTypeEnum
SpecimenCreationActivityTypeEnum.description: The high-level type of activity through which the specimen was generated (i.e. via collection from the original source, or via derivation from an existing specimen)

COLLECTION_FROM_SOURCE.description: An activity that collects an initial sample directly from a subject / source.

DERIVATION_FROM_SPECIMEN.description: An activity that derives a new specimen from an existing one.

ORCHIECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or both testicles, typically performed to treat testicular cancer or as part of prostate cancer management.

METASTASECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or more metastatic tumor deposits that have spread from a primary cancer site to distant organs or tissues.

AUTOPSY.description: Post-mortem surgical examination of a body to determine cause of death and collect tissue specimens for pathological analysis.

UNKNOWN.description: The method of specimen collection performed is not known.

ASPIRATE.description: Collection of fluid or semi-solid material by suction through a needle or syringe from a body cavity or tissue mass.

RIGHT_HEMICOLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the right portion of the colon, including the cecum, ascending colon, and part of the transverse colon.

INCISIONAL_BIOPSY.description: Surgical procedure in which a portion of a suspicious lesion or tumor is cut out for pathological examination, leaving the remainder in place.

BLOOD_DRAW.description: Collection of a blood sample via venipuncture for laboratory analysis or biospecimen banking.

PERITONEAL_LAVAGE.description: Washing of the peritoneal cavity with fluid, which is then collected and analyzed for the presence of malignant cells or other diagnostic information.

OPEN_RADICAL_PROSTATECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the prostate gland and surrounding tissues through a traditional open abdominal or perineal incision.

ABDOMINO_PERINEAL_RESECTION_OF_RECTUM.description: Surgical removal of the rectum, anus, and surrounding tissue via both abdominal and perineal incisions, typically resulting in a permanent colostomy.

SALPINGECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or both fallopian tubes, often performed to treat or reduce risk of ovarian or tubal cancer.

ENDO_RECTAL_TUMOR_RESECTION.description: Surgical excision of a rectal tumor through an endoscopic or transanal approach without requiring an external incision.

BIOPSY.description: Removal of a small sample of tissue or cells from the body for microscopic examination and pathological diagnosis.

SALPINGO_OOPHORECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or both ovaries along with the corresponding fallopian tube(s), performed to treat or prevent gynecologic cancers.

NOT_ALLOWED_TO_COLLECT.description: Specimen collection was not permitted due to regulatory, ethical, institutional, or patient consent restrictions.

WHIPPLE_PROCEDURE.description: A complex surgical procedure (pancreaticoduodenectomy) involving removal of the head of the pancreas, duodenum, gallbladder, and part of the bile duct, typically performed for pancreatic cancer.

ENUCLEATION.description: Surgical removal of a tumor or organ intact and in its entirety without cutting into the mass.

MODIFIED_RADICAL_MASTECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the entire breast along with most or all of the axillary lymph nodes, while preserving the chest wall muscles.

PARACENTESIS.description: A minimally invasive procedure in which a needle is inserted into the peritoneal cavity to drain ascitic fluid for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

OPEN_CRANIOTOMY.description: Surgical procedure involving the removal of a portion of the skull to access the brain for tumor resection or biopsy.

WEDGE_RESECTION.description: Surgical removal of a wedge-shaped section of an organ, most commonly the lung, to excise a tumor or lesion.

LAPAROSCOPIC_RADICAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Minimally invasive surgical removal of an entire kidney and surrounding tissue using a laparoscope, performed to treat renal cell carcinoma.

OPEN_PARTIAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of a tumor-bearing portion of the kidney through an open incision while preserving the remaining healthy kidney tissue.

TRANSURETHRAL_RESECTION.description: Endoscopic surgical procedure performed through the urethra to remove tumors or tissue from the bladder or prostate without external incision.

SIGMOID_COLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the sigmoid colon, the S-shaped final section of the large intestine before the rectum.

OOPHORECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or both ovaries, performed to treat ovarian cancer or as a risk-reducing measure in high-risk individuals.

TOTAL_HEPATECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the liver, typically performed in preparation for liver transplantation.

INDETERMINANT.description: The method of specimen collection could not be definitively determined from available information.

SUBTOTAL_RESECTION.description: Surgical removal of the majority but not all of a tumor or organ, leaving behind a remnant due to technical or safety constraints.

LEFT_HEMICOLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the left portion of the colon, including the descending colon and part of the transverse colon.

NEEDLE_BIOPSY.description: Minimally invasive collection of tissue or fluid using a hollow needle inserted into a lesion or mass for pathological analysis.

OTHER.description: A specimen collection method that does not correspond to any of the other specifically defined permissible values.

PANCREATECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the pancreas, performed to treat pancreatic cancer or other pancreatic neoplasms.

THORACOSCOPIC_BIOPSY.description: Minimally invasive biopsy of thoracic structures performed using a thoracoscope inserted through small chest wall incisions.

TOTAL_MASTECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the entire breast tissue including the nipple and areola, without removal of the underlying chest muscles or axillary lymph nodes.

EXCISIONAL_BIOPSY.description: Surgical removal of an entire suspicious lesion or lymph node for pathological examination, intending complete excision of the abnormal tissue.

BONE_MARROW_ASPIRATE.description: Collection of a liquid bone marrow sample via needle aspiration, typically from the iliac crest, for hematologic diagnosis and analysis.

LOCAL_RESECTION.description: Surgical removal of a tumor and a small margin of surrounding tissue, limited to the immediate area of the lesion.

LOBECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of a lobe of an organ, most commonly a lobe of the lung, to treat malignancies confined to that lobe.

FINE_NEEDLE_ASPIRATION.description: Minimally invasive diagnostic procedure using a thin needle and syringe to extract cells or fluid from a suspicious lesion for cytological analysis.

OMENTECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the omentum, the fatty tissue covering the abdominal organs, often performed during cytoreductive surgery for ovarian cancer.

TUMOR_RESECTION.description: Surgical excision of a tumor mass, with the extent of surrounding tissue removal depending on tumor type, location, and surgical intent.

CYSTECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the urinary bladder, either in part (partial) or entirety (radical), typically performed to treat bladder cancer.

GROSS_TOTAL_RESECTION.description: Surgical removal of all visually and grossly identifiable tumor tissue, as determined by the operating surgeon at the time of surgery.

THORACENTESIS.description: Minimally invasive procedure in which a needle is inserted into the pleural space to aspirate fluid for diagnostic analysis or therapeutic relief.

ANTERIOR_RESECTION_OF_RECTUM.description: Surgical removal of the upper rectum through an abdominal approach with restoration of bowel continuity, used to treat rectal cancer.

TRANSPLANT.description: Surgical procedure involving the transfer of an organ or tissue from a donor to a recipient, with specimen collection occurring at the time of organ procurement or implantation.

LAPAROSCOPIC_PARTIAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Minimally invasive removal of a tumor-bearing portion of the kidney using laparoscopic techniques, preserving the remaining renal tissue.

LAPAROSCOPIC_RADICAL_PROSTATECTOMY_WITH_.description: Complete removal of the prostate gland performed using robot-assisted laparoscopic surgery, offering enhanced precision and minimal invasiveness.

PNEUMONECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of an entire lung, typically performed to treat lung cancer confined to one lung that is not amenable to lesser resection.

PAN_PROCTO_COLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the entire colon and rectum, typically performed for colorectal cancer or inflammatory bowel disease with dysplasia.

HYSTERECTOMY_NOS.description: Surgical removal of the uterus by an unspecified or not otherwise specified technique or extent.

CORE_BIOPSY.description: Removal of a cylindrical core of tissue using a large-bore needle to obtain a histologically intact sample for pathological diagnosis.

SIMPLE_MASTECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the entire breast tissue including the nipple-areola complex, without removing axillary lymph nodes or chest wall muscles.

LUMPECTOMY.description: Breast-conserving surgery in which only the tumor and a margin of surrounding normal tissue are removed, leaving the remaining breast intact.

ENDOSCOPIC_BIOPSY.description: Tissue sampling performed through an endoscope inserted into a body cavity, allowing direct visualization and targeted biopsy of suspicious lesions.

SIMPLE_HYSTERECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the uterus and cervix without removing the parametria, upper vagina, or lymph nodes, used for benign or early-stage disease.

LYMPHADENECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of one or more lymph nodes for pathological examination or as part of cancer staging and treatment.

LAPAROSCOPIC_BIOPSY.description: Tissue sampling performed using a laparoscope introduced through small abdominal incisions, allowing visualization and biopsy of intraperitoneal structures.

TUMOR_DEBULKING.description: Cytoreductive surgery aimed at removing as much tumor mass as possible when complete resection is not feasible, to reduce tumor burden and improve treatment response.

ASCITES_DRAINAGE.description: Therapeutic or diagnostic removal of accumulated fluid from the peritoneal cavity, which may be examined for malignant cells.

ENDOSCOPIC_MUCOSAL_RESECTION.description: Endoscopic technique for removing superficial tumors or lesions confined to the mucosal layer of the gastrointestinal tract.

LAPAROSCOPIC_RADICAL_PROSTATECTOMY_WITHO.description: Complete removal of the prostate gland using standard laparoscopic techniques without robotic assistance.

TOTAL_COLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the entire large intestine (colon) while preserving the rectum, typically performed for colorectal cancer or hereditary polyposis syndromes.

LIQUID_BIOPSY.description: Non-invasive sampling of circulating tumor cells, cell-free DNA, or other cancer biomarkers from a body fluid, most commonly blood.

RADICAL_HYSTERECTOMY.description: Extensive surgical removal of the uterus, cervix, upper vagina, parametria, and regional lymph nodes, performed for gynecologic malignancies such as cervical cancer.

SURGICAL_RESECTION.description: Surgical removal of a tumor or diseased tissue, used as a general term when the specific resection type is not further specified.

OPEN_RADICAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Complete removal of a kidney, adrenal gland, and surrounding fatty tissue through a traditional open incision, performed to treat renal cell carcinoma.

TRANSVERSE_COLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the transverse colon, the middle horizontal portion of the large intestine connecting the ascending and descending colon.

HAND_ASSISTED_LAPAROSCOPIC_RADICAL_NEPHR.description: Laparoscopic radical nephrectomy performed with the aid of a hand port that allows direct manual assistance during the minimally invasive procedure.

PUNCH_BIOPSY.description: Removal of a small cylindrical core of skin or mucosal tissue using a circular cutting instrument for dermatological or mucosal pathological diagnosis.

PARTIAL_HEPATECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of a portion of the liver, performed to resect primary liver tumors or metastatic deposits while preserving adequate hepatic function.

SUPRACERVICAL_HYSTERECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the uterine body while leaving the cervix in place, also known as a subtotal hysterectomy.

OTHER_SURGICAL_RESECTION.description: A surgical resection procedure that does not correspond to any of the other specifically defined resection types.

NOT_REPORTED.description: The method of specimen collection was not documented or reported in the available records.

FULL_HYSTERECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the uterus and cervix, sometimes also including the ovaries and fallopian tubes, to treat uterine or cervical malignancies.

TONSILLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the palatine tonsils, performed for tonsillar tumors or as part of treatment for oropharyngeal cancer.

SUPRAGLOTTIC_LARYNGECTOMY.description: Partial removal of the larynx above the vocal cords (glottis), used to treat supraglottic laryngeal cancer while preserving voice function.

SUPERFICIAL_PAROTIDECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the superficial lobe of the parotid gland, typically performed to excise parotid tumors located superficial to the facial nerve.

LARYNGOPHARYNGECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the larynx and part or all of the pharynx, performed for advanced cancers involving both structures.

MAXILLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the maxilla (upper jaw), performed to treat malignant tumors of the hard palate, maxillary sinus, or upper jaw.

PARTIAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Nephron-sparing surgery in which only the tumor-bearing portion of the kidney is removed, preserving the remaining healthy renal parenchyma.

MANDIBULECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the mandible (lower jaw), performed to treat malignant tumors involving the mandible or adjacent oral cavity.

BUCCAL_MUCOSAL_RESECTION.description: Surgical excision of tumor-bearing tissue from the buccal mucosa (inner lining of the cheek), performed to treat oral cavity cancers.

VERTICAL_HEMILARYNGECTOMY.description: Partial laryngectomy involving vertical removal of one side of the larynx, used to treat glottic cancers while preserving voice and swallowing function.

TOTAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of an entire kidney, performed to treat renal malignancies or other kidney diseases.

TOTAL_LARYNGECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the larynx, resulting in permanent loss of natural voice, performed for advanced laryngeal cancer.

TRANSORAL_LASER_EXCISION.description: Minimally invasive surgical technique using a laser delivered through the mouth to precisely excise laryngeal or pharyngeal tumors without external incision.

PAROTIDECTOMY_NOS.description: Surgical removal of the parotid gland by an unspecified or not otherwise specified extent or technique.

RADICAL_MAXILLECTOMY.description: Extensive surgical removal of the entire maxilla along with surrounding structures, performed for aggressive or extensive malignant tumors of the upper jaw.

ENDOLARYNGEAL_EXCISION.description: Surgical removal of a laryngeal lesion performed entirely through the mouth using endoscopic instruments, without external neck incision.

PALATECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the palate, performed to treat malignant tumors involving the hard or soft palate.

GLOSSECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of all or part of the tongue, performed to treat oral tongue or base of tongue malignancies.

PARTIAL_LARYNGECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of a portion of the larynx while preserving remaining laryngeal function, used for early to intermediate-stage laryngeal cancers.

LYMPH_NODE_DISSECTION.description: Systematic surgical removal of a group of regional lymph nodes for pathological staging or therapeutic management of cancer spread.

RADICAL_PROSTATECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the prostate gland, seminal vesicles, and surrounding tissue, performed to treat localized prostate cancer.

DEEP_PAROTIDECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of the deep lobe of the parotid gland, performed for tumors located deep to the facial nerve.

SUBTOTAL_PROSTATECTOMY.description: Partial surgical removal of the prostate gland, in which only a portion of the gland is excised rather than the complete organ.

RADICAL_NEPHRECTOMY.description: Complete surgical removal of the kidney along with the adrenal gland, surrounding fatty tissue, and regional lymph nodes, performed for renal cell carcinoma.

SUPRACRICOID_LARYNGECTOMY.description: Partial laryngectomy involving removal of the supracricoid structures while preserving the cricoid cartilage, used for select intermediate-stage laryngeal cancers.

PARTIAL_MAXILLECTOMY.description: Surgical removal of a portion of the maxilla, performed for localized malignant or benign tumors that do not require total maxillary resection.

SpecimenQualityObservationTypeEnum.name: SpecimenQualityObservationTypeEnum
SpecimenQualityObservationTypeEnum.description: Types of measurements that reflect the quality of a specimen or its suitability for use.

A260_A280_RATIO.description: Ratio of absorbance measured at a wavelength of 260 over that at a wavelength of 280.

RIBOSOMAL_RNA_28S_16S_RATIO.description: Ratio of quantity of 28s RNA over that of 16s RNA.

SpecimenQualityObservationMethodEnum.name: SpecimenQualityObservationMethodEnum
SpecimenQualityObservationMethodEnum.description: A type of method used in determining the quantity of a specimen.

UV_SPEC.description: A technique used to measure light absorbance across the ultraviolet and visible ranges of the electromagnetic spectrum.

PICO_GREEN.description: A technique applying the Pico488 fluorescent sensor dye that is used for quantifying the amount of double-stranded DNA (dsDNA) present in a given sample.

SpecimenQuantityObservationTypeEnum.name: SpecimenQuantityObservationTypeEnum
SpecimenQuantityObservationTypeEnum.description: Measures related to the quantity of a specimen or analyte it currently contains - e.g. its weight, volume, or concentration.

WEIGHT.description: The current weight of the specimen, at the time of recording (as opposed to an initial weight before its processing or portioning).

VOLUME.description: The current total volume of the specimen, at the time of recording.

CONCENTRATION.description: The concentration of an extracted analyte that is present in a specimen (specifically, in a specimen of type 'analyte', or an 'aliquot' derived from an analyte). For example, the concentration of DNA in a specimen created through extracting DNA from a blood sample.

SpecimenProcessingActivityTypeEnum.name: SpecimenProcessingActivityTypeEnum
SpecimenProcessingActivityTypeEnum.description: The high-level type of processing activity performed.

FIXATION.description: A processing activity that applies chemicals to preserve biological tissues from decay due to autolysis or putrefaction

FREEZING.description: A processing activity that aims to freeze a specimen.

MOUNTING.description: A processing activity that aims to secure a specimen or slide in place in preparation for further examination (usually via microscopy)

PRESERVATION.description: A processing activity that aims to preserve a specimen.

UnitOfMeasurementEnum.name: UnitOfMeasurementEnum
UnitOfMeasurementEnum.description: Standard units of measurement from the [Units of Measurement (UOM) ontology](https://units-of-measurement.org/). Units-of-measurement (UOM) provides URLs for Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM) codes, and mappings to a number of units ontologies and systems, in human- and machine-readable linked data formats.

GravityDomainEnum.name: GravityDomainEnum
GravityDomainEnum.description: Social Determinants of Health domains as defined in the Gravity Project

DIGITAL_ACCESS.description: The ability to access and use digital technologies, including internet connectivity and devices.

DIGITAL_LITERACY.description: The capacity to find, evaluate, and communicate information using digital technology.

EDUCATIONAL_ATTAINMENT.description: The highest level of formal education an individual has completed.

ELDER_ABUSE.description: Physical, emotional, financial, or other mistreatment or neglect of older adults.

EMPLOYMENT_STATUS.description: An individual's current relationship to the workforce, including employment, unemployment, or underemployment.

FINANCIAL_INSECURITY.description: Inadequate financial resources to meet basic needs and maintain economic stability.

FOOD_INSECURITY.description: Limited or uncertain access to adequate, nutritious food due to financial or other constraints.

HEALTH_INSURANCE_COVERAGE_STATUS.description: Whether an individual has health insurance and the type or adequacy of that coverage.

HEALTH_LITERACY.description: The degree to which individuals can obtain, process, and understand basic health information to make appropriate health decisions.

HOMELESSNESS.description: The condition of lacking a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence.

HOUSING_INSTABILITY.description: Difficulty maintaining stable housing, including risk of eviction, frequent moves, or inability to pay rent or mortgage.

INADEQUATE_HOUSING.description: Living conditions that are unsafe, overcrowded, or otherwise fail to meet basic habitability standards.

INTIMATE_PARTNER_VIOLENCE_IPV.description: Physical, sexual, psychological, or economic abuse perpetrated by a current or former intimate partner.

MATERIAL_HARDSHIP.description: Inability to meet basic material needs such as food, housing, clothing, or medical care.

MEDICAL_COST_BURDEN.description: The financial strain caused by out-of-pocket medical expenses relative to an individual's or household's income.

SOCIAL_CONNECTION.description: The degree to which an individual has relationships and social support networks that promote well-being.

STRESS.description: Psychological or physical tension arising from adverse or demanding circumstances that affects health and well-being.

TRANSPORTATION_INSECURITY.description: Lack of reliable, safe, or affordable transportation needed to meet daily needs and access services.

UTILITY_INSECURITY.description: Inability to reliably afford or access essential utilities such as electricity, heat, or water.

VETERAN_STATUS.description: Whether an individual has served in the military, which may affect eligibility for benefits and expose them to unique health risks.

ConditionSeverityEnum.name: ConditionSeverityEnum
ConditionSeverityEnum.description: The clinical severity level assigned to a condition or diagnosis.

MILD.description: A condition that causes minimal symptoms or functional impairment and typically does not require intensive intervention.

MODERATE.description: A condition that causes noticeable symptoms or functional impairment and may require active treatment or monitoring.

SEVERE.description: A condition that causes significant symptoms or functional impairment, often requiring urgent or intensive intervention.

FamilyRelationshipEnum.name: FamilyRelationshipEnum
FamilyRelationshipEnum.description: Values describing the relationship between an individual and family members.

NATURAL_PARENT.description: A relation to a natural parent (mother or father) when otherwise not specified.

NATURAL_FATHER.description: A relation to a natural father.

NATURAL_MOTHER.description: A relation to a natural mother.

NATURAL_SIBLING.description: A relation to a natural sibling (sister or brother) when otherwise not specified.

NATURAL_BROTHER.description: A relation to a natural brother.

NATURAL_SISTER.description: A relation to a natural sister.

NATURAL_CHILD.description: A relation to a natural child.

MeasurementObservationTypeEnum.name: MeasurementObservationTypeEnum
MeasurementObservationTypeEnum.description: Values describing the types of MeasurementObservations.

EducationalAttainmentObservationTypeEnum.name: EducationalAttainmentObservationTypeEnum
EducationalAttainmentObservationTypeEnum.description: Values describing the types of Education Attainment observed in an Observation.

SmokingStatusObservationTypeEnum.name: SmokingStatusObservationTypeEnum
SmokingStatusObservationTypeEnum.description: Values describing the types of Smoking Status observed in an Observation.

CauseOfDeathEnum.name: CauseOfDeathEnum
CauseOfDeathEnum.description: A constrained set of enumerative values containing the ICD-10 diagnoses.

ResearchProjectTypeEnum.name: ResearchProjectTypeEnum
ResearchProjectTypeEnum.description: Values describing types of research projects.

BodyPositionEnum.name: BodyPositionEnum
BodyPositionEnum.description: Values describing the body position of a patient during a measurement or observation.

SITTING_POSITION.description: The patient is in a sitting position during the measurement or observation.

ORTHOSTATIC_BODY_POSITION.description: The patient is in a standing position during the measurement or observation.

SUPINE_BODY_POSITION.description: The patient is lying on their back during the measurement or observation.

PRONE_BODY_POSITION.description: The patient is lying on their stomach during the measurement or observation.

BaseObservationTypeEnum.name: BaseObservationTypeEnum
BaseObservationTypeEnum.description: Values describing the types of an Observation.

float.name: float
float.description: A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification

decimal.name: decimal
decimal.description: A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal specification

datetime.name: datetime
datetime.description: The combination of a date and time
